revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{神社| 名称=宿奈川田神社| 所在地=大阪府柏原市高井田661番地| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|15|N|135|38|16|E|scale:30000}}| ...'
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=宿奈川田神社| 所在地=大阪府柏原市高井田661番地| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|15|N|135|38|16|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=宿奈彦根命
高皇産霊命
級戸辺命| 社格=式内社（小）・村社| 本殿=[[流造]]| 例祭=[[7月23日]]| 別名=白坂大明神}} '''宿奈川田神社'''（すくなかわたじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[柏原市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 [[スクナビコナ|宿奈彦根命]]・[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊命]]・[[シナツヒコ|級戸辺命]]を主祭神とする。『神社要録』や『特選神名牒』では「祭神不詳」となっている。 ==歴史== 創建の由緒は不詳である。近くにある[[天湯川田神社]]（柏原市高井田89）とともに、当地を拠点としていた鳥取連が[[祖神]]を祀ったものとみられる。[[弘仁式]]の時から[[官社]]となっており、[[延喜式神名帳]]に「河内国大県郡 宿奈川田神社」と記載され、小社に列している。 中世には「白坂大明神」と称していた。一説には、現在[[二宮神社 (柏原市)|二宮神社]]（柏原市安堂町19-9。大正時代に2社を合祀して現在地に遷座）のある地が「字白坂」であることから、元の宿奈川田神社の鎮座地であるという。そのため、二宮神社も式内・宿奈川田神社の論社とされているが、二宮神社では式内社とは称していない。 明治5年（[[1872年]]）に村社に列格し、白坂明神から現社名に改称した。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.geocities.jp/engishiki01/kawachi/html/040402-01.html 宿奈川田神社]（延喜式神社の調査） {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなかわたしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:柏原市]]

revision.comment: 写真1枚提供ほか
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=宿奈川田神社| 画像=[[Image:Sukunakawata Shrine01.jpg|260px]]
宿奈川田神社（白坂神社）境内| 所在地=大阪府柏原市高井田661番地| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|15|N|135|38|16|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=宿奈彦根命
高皇産霊命
級戸辺命| 社格=式内社（小）・村社| 本殿=[[流造]]| 例祭=[[7月23日]]| 別名=白坂大明神}} '''宿奈川田神社'''（すくなかわたじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[柏原市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。地元では通称 '''白坂神社'''（しらさかじんじゃ）と呼ばれ、地図や看板でもそのように表記されている。 [[スクナビコナ|宿奈彦根命]]・[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊命]]・[[シナツヒコ|級戸辺命]]を主祭神とする。『神社要録』や『特選神名牒』では「祭神不詳」となっている。 ==歴史== 創建の由緒は不詳である。近くにある[[天湯川田神社]]（柏原市高井田89）とともに、当地を拠点としていた鳥取連が[[祖神]]を祀ったものとみられる。[[弘仁式]]の時から[[官社]]となっており、[[延喜式神名帳]]に「河内国大県郡 宿奈川田神社」と記載され、小社に列している。 中世には「白坂大明神」と称していた。一説には、現在[[二宮神社 (柏原市)|二宮神社]]（柏原市安堂町19-9。大正時代に2社を合祀して現在地に遷座）のある地が「字白坂」であることから、元の宿奈川田神社の鎮座地であるという。そのため、二宮神社も式内・宿奈川田神社の論社とされているが、二宮神社では式内社とは称していない。 明治5年（[[1872年]]）に村社に列格し、白坂明神から現社名に改称した。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.geocities.jp/engishiki01/kawachi/html/040402-01.html 宿奈川田神社]（延喜式神社の調査） {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなかわたしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:柏原市]]

[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊命]]
[[シナツヒコ|級戸辺命]]| 社格=[[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]]| 本殿=[[流造]]| 例祭=[[7月23日]]| 別名=白坂大明神}} '''宿奈川田神社'''（すくなかわたじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[柏原市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。地元では通称 '''白坂神社'''（しらさかじんじゃ）と呼ばれ、地図や看板でもそのように表記されている。 ==祭神== [[スクナビコナ|宿奈彦根命]]・[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊命]]・[[シナツヒコ|級戸辺命]]を主祭神とする。『[[神社覈録]]』や『[[特選神名牒]]』では「祭神不詳」となっている。 ==歴史== 創建の由緒は不詳である。近くにある[[天湯川田神社]]（柏原市高井田89）とともに、当地を拠点としていた鳥取連が[[祖神]]を祀ったものとみられる。[[弘仁式]]の時から[[官社]]となっており、[[延喜式神名帳]]に「河内国大県郡 宿奈川田神社」と記載され、小社に列している。 中世には「白坂大明神」と称していた。一説には、現在[[二宮神社 (柏原市)|二宮神社]]（柏原市安堂町19-9。大正時代に2社を合祀して現在地に遷座）のある地が「字白坂」であることから、元の宿奈川田神社の鎮座地であるという。そのため、二宮神社も式内・宿奈川田神社の論社とされているが、二宮神社では式内社とは称していない。 明治5年（[[1872年]]）に村社に列格し、白坂明神から現社名に改称した。 {{Commons|Category:Sukunakawata-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなかわたしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]]

旧[[村社]] | 創建=不詳 | 本殿=[[流造]] | 例祭=[[7月23日]] | 別名=白坂大明神 }} [[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''宿奈川田神社'''（すくなかわたじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[柏原市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。地元では「白坂神社（しらさかじんじゃ）」と通称され、地図や看板でもそのように表記されている。 ==祭神== [[スクナビコナ|宿奈彦根命]]・[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊命]]・[[シナツヒコ|級戸辺命]]を主祭神とする。『[[神社覈録]]』や『[[特選神名牒]]』では「祭神不詳」となっている。 ==歴史== 創建の由緒は不詳である。近くにある[[天湯川田神社]]（柏原市高井田89）とともに、当地を拠点としていた鳥取連が[[祖神]]を祀ったものとみられる。『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「天神本紀」では[[少彦根命]]を鳥取連の祖神としており、「三嶋」系図[[鈴木真年]]「三嶋」『百家系図』巻三十七。では鳥取連の祖・[[天湯川田命]]の子を少彦根命と伝える。[[弘仁式]]の時から[[官社]]となっており、[[延喜式神名帳]]に「河内国大県郡 宿奈川田神社」と記載され、小社に列している。 中世には「白坂大明神」と称していた。一説には、現在[[二宮神社 (柏原市)|二宮神社]]（柏原市安堂町19-9。大正時代に2社を合祀して現在地に遷座）のある地が「字白坂」であることから、元の宿奈川田神社の鎮座地であるという。そのため、二宮神社も式内・宿奈川田神社の論社とされているが、二宮神社では式内社とは称していない。 明治5年（[[1872年]]）に村社に列格し、白坂明神から現社名に改称した。 ==境内== File:Sukunakawata-jinja, honden.jpg|本殿 File:Sukunakawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|末社 ==交通アクセス== *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]] [[関西本線|大和路線]] [[高井田駅 (大阪府柏原市)|高井田駅]]（徒歩すぐ） ==関連項目== {{Commonscat|Sukunakawata-jinja}} *[[天湯川田神社]] *[[河内国の式内社一覧]] {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなかわたしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]]

[[シナツヒコ|級戸辺命]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] | 創建=不詳 | 本殿=[[流造]] | 例祭=[[7月23日]] | 別名=白坂大明神 | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=宿奈
川田神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''宿奈川田神社'''（すくなかわたじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[柏原市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。地元では「白坂神社（しらさかじんじゃ）」と通称され、地図や看板でもそのように表記されている。 ==祭神== [[スクナビコナ|宿奈彦根命]]・[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊命]]・[[シナツヒコ|級戸辺命]]を主祭神とする。『[[神社覈録]]』や『[[特選神名牒]]』では「祭神不詳」となっている。 ==歴史== 創建の由緒は不詳である。近くにある[[天湯川田神社]]（柏原市高井田89）とともに、当地を拠点としていた鳥取連が[[祖神]]を祀ったものとみられる。『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「天神本紀」では[[少彦根命]]を鳥取連の祖神としている。[[弘仁式]]の時から[[官社]]となっており、[[延喜式神名帳]]に「河内国大県郡 宿奈川田神社」と記載され、小社に列している。 中世には「白坂大明神」と称していた。一説には、現在[[二宮神社 (柏原市)|二宮神社]]（柏原市安堂町19-9。大正時代に2社を合祀して現在地に遷座）のある地が「字白坂」であることから、元の宿奈川田神社の鎮座地であるという。そのため、二宮神社も式内・宿奈川田神社の論社とされているが、二宮神社では式内社とは称していない。 明治5年（[[1872年]]）に村社に列格し、白坂明神から現社名に改称した。 ==境内== File:Sukunakawata-jinja, honden.jpg|本殿 File:Sukunakawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|末社 ==交通アクセス== *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]] [[関西本線|大和路線]] [[高井田駅 (大阪府柏原市)|高井田駅]]（徒歩すぐ） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==関連項目== {{Commonscat|Sukunakawata-jinja}} *[[天湯川田神社]] *[[河内国の式内社一覧]] {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなかわたしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]]

{{nihongo|'''Sukunakawata Shrine'''|宿奈川田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sukunakawata Shrine'''|宿奈川田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sukunakawata Shrine'''|宿奈川田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sukunakawata Shrine'''|宿奈川田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sukunakawata Shrine'''|宿奈川田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sukunakawata Shrine'''|宿奈川田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sukunakawata Shrine'''|宿奈川田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sukunakawata Shrine'''|宿奈川田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sukunakawata Shrine'''|宿奈川田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sukunakawata Shrine'''|宿奈川田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sukunakawata Shrine'''|宿奈川田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sukunakawata Shrine'''|宿奈川田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sukunakawata Shrine'''|宿奈川田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sukunakawata Shrine'''|宿奈川田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

revision.comment: 20 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} {{神社 | 名称=宿奈川田神社 | 画像=[[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[大阪府]][[柏原市]]高井田661番地 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[スクナビコナ|宿奈彦根命]]
[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊命]]
[[シナツヒコ|級戸辺命]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] | 創建=不詳 | 本殿=[[流造]] | 例祭=[[7月23日]] | 別名=白坂大明神 | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=宿奈
川田神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''宿奈川田神社'''（すくなかわたじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[柏原市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。地元では「白坂神社（しらさかじんじゃ）」と通称され、地図や看板でもそのように表記されている。 ==祭神== [[スクナビコナ|宿奈彦根命]]・[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊命]]・[[シナツヒコ|級戸辺命]]を主祭神とする。『[[神社覈録]]』や『[[特選神名牒]]』では「祭神不詳」となっている。 ==歴史== 創建の由緒は不詳である。近くにある[[天湯川田神社]]（柏原市高井田89）とともに、当地を拠点としていた鳥取連が[[祖神]]を祀ったものとみられる。『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「天神本紀」では[[少彦根命]]を鳥取連の祖神としている。[[弘仁式]]の時から[[官社]]となっており、[[延喜式神名帳]]に「河内国大県郡 宿奈川田神社」と記載され、小社に列している。 中世には「白坂大明神」と称していた。一説には、現在[[二宮神社 (柏原市)|二宮神社]]（柏原市安堂町19-9。大正時代に2社を合祀して現在地に遷座）のある地が「字白坂」であることから、元の宿奈川田神社の鎮座地であるという。そのため、二宮神社も式内・宿奈川田神社の論社とされているが、二宮神社では式内社とは称していない。 明治5年（[[1872年]]）に村社に列格し、白坂明神から現社名に改称した。 ==境内== File:Sukunakawata-jinja, honden.jpg|本殿 File:Sukunakawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|末社 ==交通アクセス== *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]] [[関西本線|大和路線]] [[高井田駅 (大阪府柏原市)|高井田駅]]（徒歩すぐ） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==関連項目== {{Commonscat|Sukunakawata-jinja}} *[[天湯川田神社]] *[[河内国の式内社一覧]] {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなかわたしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]]

川田神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''宿奈川田神社'''（すくなかわたじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[柏原市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。地元では「白坂神社（しらさかじんじゃ）」と通称され、地図や看板でもそのように表記されている。 ==祭神== [[スクナビコナ|宿奈彦根命]]・[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊命]]・[[シナツヒコ|級戸辺命]]を主祭神とする。『[[神社覈録]]』や『[[特選神名牒]]』では「祭神不詳」となっている。 ==歴史== 創建の由緒は不詳である。近くにある[[天湯川田神社]]（柏原市高井田89）とともに、当地を拠点としていた鳥取連が[[祖神]]を祀ったものとみられる。『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「天神本紀」では[[少彦根命]]を鳥取連の祖神としている。[[弘仁式]]の時から[[官社]]となっており、[[延喜式神名帳]]に「河内国大県郡 宿奈川田神社」と記載され、小社に列している。 中世には「白坂大明神」と称していた。一説には、現在[[二宮神社 (柏原市)|二宮神社]]（柏原市安堂町19-9。大正時代に2社を合祀して現在地に遷座）のある地が「字白坂」であることから、元の宿奈川田神社の鎮座地であるという。そのため、二宮神社も式内・宿奈川田神社の論社とされているが、二宮神社では式内社とは称していない。 明治5年（[[1872年]]）に村社に列格し、白坂明神から現社名に改称した。 ==境内== File:Sukunakawata-jinja, honden.jpg|本殿 File:Sukunakawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|末社 ==交通アクセス== *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]] [[関西本線|大和路線]] [[高井田駅 (大阪府柏原市)|高井田駅]]（徒歩すぐ） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==関連項目== {{Commonscat|Sukunakawata-jinja}} *[[天湯川田神社]] *[[河内国の式内社一覧]] {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなかわたしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]] ==End Japanese==

川田神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''宿奈川田神社'''（すくなかわたじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[柏原市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。地元では「白坂神社（しらさかじんじゃ）」と通称され、地図や看板でもそのように表記されている。 ==祭神== [[スクナビコナ|宿奈彦根命]]・[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊命]]・[[シナツヒコ|級戸辺命]]を主祭神とする。『[[神社覈録]]』や『[[特選神名牒]]』では「祭神不詳」となっている。 ==歴史== 創建の由緒は不詳である。近くにある[[天湯川田神社]]（柏原市高井田89）とともに、当地を拠点としていた鳥取連が[[祖神]]を祀ったものとみられる。『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「天神本紀」では[[少彦根命]]を鳥取連の祖神としている。[[弘仁式]]の時から[[官社]]となっており、[[延喜式神名帳]]に「河内国大県郡 宿奈川田神社」と記載され、小社に列している。 中世には「白坂大明神」と称していた。一説には、現在[[二宮神社 (柏原市)|二宮神社]]（柏原市安堂町19-9。大正時代に2社を合祀して現在地に遷座）のある地が「字白坂」であることから、元の宿奈川田神社の鎮座地であるという。そのため、二宮神社も式内・宿奈川田神社の論社とされているが、二宮神社では式内社とは称していない。 明治5年（[[1872年]]）に村社に列格し、白坂明神から現社名に改称した。 ==境内== File:Sukunakawata-jinja, honden.jpg|本殿 File:Sukunakawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|末社 ==交通アクセス== *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]] [[関西本線|大和路線]] [[高井田駅 (大阪府柏原市)|高井田駅]]（徒歩すぐ） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==関連項目== {{Commonscat|Sukunakawata-jinja}} *[[天湯川田神社]] *[[河内国の式内社一覧]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]] ==End Japanese==

川田神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''宿奈川田神社'''（すくなかわたじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[柏原市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。地元では「白坂神社（しらさかじんじゃ）」と通称され、地図や看板でもそのように表記されている。 ==祭神== [[スクナビコナ|宿奈彦根命]]・[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊命]]・[[シナツヒコ|級戸辺命]]を主祭神とする。『[[神社覈録]]』や『[[特選神名牒]]』では「祭神不詳」となっている。 ==歴史== 創建の由緒は不詳である。近くにある[[天湯川田神社]]（柏原市高井田89）とともに、当地を拠点としていた鳥取連が[[祖神]]を祀ったものとみられる。『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「天神本紀」では[[少彦根命]]を鳥取連の祖神としている。[[弘仁式]]の時から[[官社]]となっており、[[延喜式神名帳]]に「河内国大県郡 宿奈川田神社」と記載され、小社に列している。 中世には「白坂大明神」と称していた。一説には、現在[[二宮神社 (柏原市)|二宮神社]]（柏原市安堂町19-9。大正時代に2社を合祀して現在地に遷座）のある地が「字白坂」であることから、元の宿奈川田神社の鎮座地であるという。そのため、二宮神社も式内・宿奈川田神社の論社とされているが、二宮神社では式内社とは称していない。 明治5年（[[1872年]]）に村社に列格し、白坂明神から現社名に改称した。 ==境内== File:Sukunakawata-jinja, honden.jpg|本殿 File:Sukunakawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|末社 ==交通アクセス== *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]] [[関西本線|大和路線]] [[高井田駅 (大阪府柏原市)|高井田駅]]（徒歩すぐ） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==関連項目== {{Commonscat|Sukunakawata-jinja}} *[[天湯川田神社]] *[[河内国の式内社一覧]] ==End Japanese== [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]]

川田神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''宿奈川田神社'''（すくなかわたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|柏原市|Q873583}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社}}。地元では「白坂神社（しらさかじんじゃ）」と通称され、地図や看板でもそのように表記されている。 ==祭神== {{jalink|スクナビコナ|Q3275687|宿奈彦根命}}・{{jalink|タカミムスビ||高皇産霊命}}・{{jalink|シナツヒコ|Q1073925|級戸辺命}}を主祭神とする。『{{jalink|神社覈録|Q11590708}}』や『{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092}}』では「祭神不詳」となっている。 ==歴史== 創建の由緒は不詳である。近くにある{{jalink|天湯川田神社|Q63891125}}（柏原市高井田89）とともに、当地を拠点としていた鳥取連が{{jalink|祖神|Q85882782}}を祀ったものとみられる。『{{jalink|先代旧事本紀|Q2716751}}』「天神本紀」では{{jalink|少彦根命}}を鳥取連の祖神としている。{{jalink|弘仁式|Q17228444}}の時から{{jalink|官社}}となっており、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に「河内国大県郡 宿奈川田神社」と記載され、小社に列している。 中世には「白坂大明神」と称していた。一説には、現在{{jalink|二宮神社 (柏原市)||二宮神社}}（柏原市安堂町19-9。大正時代に2社を合祀して現在地に遷座）のある地が「字白坂」であることから、元の宿奈川田神社の鎮座地であるという。そのため、二宮神社も式内・宿奈川田神社の論社とされているが、二宮神社では式内社とは称していない。 明治5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）に村社に列格し、白坂明神から現社名に改称した。 ==境内== File:Sukunakawata-jinja, honden.jpg|本殿 File:Sukunakawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|末社 ==交通アクセス== *{{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125|JR西日本}} {{jalink|関西本線|Q1199703|大和路線}} {{jalink|高井田駅 (大阪府柏原市)|Q856508|高井田駅}}（徒歩すぐ） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==関連項目== {{Commonscat|Sukunakawata-jinja}} *{{jalink|天湯川田神社|Q63891125}} *{{jalink|河内国の式内社一覧|Q11553385}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]]

'大阪府柏原市高井田661番地', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sukunakawata-jinja}} [[JA:宿奈川田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11455616}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} {{神社 | 名称=宿奈川田神社 | 画像=[[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|柏原市|Q873583}}高井田661番地 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|スクナビコナ|Q3275687|宿奈彦根命}}
{{jalink|タカミムスビ||高皇産霊命}}
{{jalink|シナツヒコ|Q1073925|級戸辺命}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} | 創建=不詳 | 本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|7月23日|Q2722}} | 別名=白坂大明神 | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=宿奈
川田神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''宿奈川田神社'''（すくなかわたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|柏原市|Q873583}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。地元では「白坂神社（しらさかじんじゃ）」と通称され、地図や看板でもそのように表記されている。 ===祭神=== {{jalink|スクナビコナ|Q3275687|宿奈彦根命}}・{{jalink|タカミムスビ||高皇産霊命}}・{{jalink|シナツヒコ|Q1073925|級戸辺命}}を主祭神とする。『{{jalink|神社覈録|Q11590708}}』や『{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092}}』では「祭神不詳」となっている。 ===歴史=== 創建の由緒は不詳である。近くにある{{jalink|天湯川田神社|Q63891125}}（柏原市高井田89）とともに、当地を拠点としていた鳥取連が{{jalink|祖神|Q85882782}}を祀ったものとみられる。『{{jalink|先代旧事本紀|Q2716751}}』「天神本紀」では{{jalink|少彦根命}}を鳥取連の祖神としている。{{jalink|弘仁式|Q17228444}}の時から{{jalink|官社}}となっており、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に「河内国大県郡 宿奈川田神社」と記載され、小社に列している。 中世には「白坂大明神」と称していた。一説には、現在{{jalink|二宮神社 (柏原市)||二宮神社}}（柏原市安堂町19-9。大正時代に2社を合祀して現在地に遷座）のある地が「字白坂」であることから、元の宿奈川田神社の鎮座地であるという。そのため、二宮神社も式内・宿奈川田神社の論社とされているが、二宮神社では式内社とは称していない。 明治5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）に村社に列格し、白坂明神から現社名に改称した。 ===境内=== File:Sukunakawata-jinja, honden.jpg|本殿 File:Sukunakawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|末社 ===交通アクセス=== *{{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125|JR西日本}} {{jalink|関西本線|Q1199703|大和路線}} {{jalink|高井田駅 (大阪府柏原市)|Q856508|高井田駅}}（徒歩すぐ） === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===関連項目=== {{Commonscat|Sukunakawata-jinja}} *{{jalink|天湯川田神社|Q63891125}} *{{jalink|河内国の式内社一覧|Q11553385}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなかわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]]

旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} | 創建=不詳 | 本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|7月23日|Q2722}} | 別名=白坂大明神 | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=宿奈
川田神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''宿奈川田神社'''（すくなかわたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|柏原市|Q873583}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。地元では「白坂神社（しらさかじんじゃ）」と通称され、地図や看板でもそのように表記されている。 ===祭神=== {{jalink|スクナビコナ|Q3275687|宿奈彦根命}}・{{jalink|タカミムスビ||高皇産霊命}}・{{jalink|シナツヒコ|Q1073925|級戸辺命}}を主祭神とする。『{{jalink|神社覈録|Q11590708}}』や『{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092}}』では「祭神不詳」となっている。 ===歴史=== 創建の由緒は不詳である。近くにある{{jalink|天湯川田神社|Q63891125}}（柏原市高井田89）とともに、当地を拠点としていた鳥取連が{{jalink|祖神|Q85882782}}を祀ったものとみられる。『{{jalink|先代旧事本紀|Q2716751}}』「天神本紀」では{{jalink|少彦根命}}を鳥取連の祖神としている。{{jalink|弘仁式|Q17228444}}の時から{{jalink|官社}}となっており、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に「河内国大県郡 宿奈川田神社」と記載され、小社に列している。 中世には「白坂大明神」と称していた。一説には、現在{{jalink|二宮神社 (柏原市)||二宮神社}}（柏原市安堂町19-9。大正時代に2社を合祀して現在地に遷座）のある地が「字白坂」であることから、元の宿奈川田神社の鎮座地であるという。そのため、二宮神社も式内・宿奈川田神社の論社とされているが、二宮神社では式内社とは称していない。 明治5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）に村社に列格し、白坂明神から現社名に改称した。 ===境内=== File:Sukunakawata-jinja, honden.jpg|本殿 File:Sukunakawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|末社 ===交通アクセス=== *{{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125|JR西日本}} {{jalink|関西本線|Q1199703|大和路線}} {{jalink|高井田駅 (大阪府柏原市)|Q856508|高井田駅}}（徒歩すぐ） === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===関連項目=== {{Commonscat|Sukunakawata-jinja}} *{{jalink|天湯川田神社|Q63891125}} *{{jalink|河内国の式内社一覧|Q11553385}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなかわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

川田神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''宿奈川田神社'''（すくなかわたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|柏原市|Q873583}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。地元では「白坂神社（しらさかじんじゃ）」と通称され、地図や看板でもそのように表記されている。 ===祭神=== {{jalink|スクナビコナ|Q3275687|宿奈彦根命}}・{{jalink|タカミムスビ||高皇産霊命}}・{{jalink|シナツヒコ|Q1073925|級戸辺命}}を主祭神とする。『{{jalink|神社覈録|Q11590708}}』や『{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092}}』では「祭神不詳」となっている。 ===歴史=== 創建の由緒は不詳である。近くにある{{jalink|天湯川田神社|Q63891125}}（柏原市高井田89）とともに、当地を拠点としていた鳥取連が{{jalink|祖神|Q85882782}}を祀ったものとみられる。『{{jalink|先代旧事本紀|Q2716751}}』「天神本紀」では{{jalink|少彦根命}}を鳥取連の祖神としている。{{jalink|弘仁式|Q17228444}}の時から{{jalink|官社}}となっており、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に「河内国大県郡 宿奈川田神社」と記載され、小社に列している。 中世には「白坂大明神」と称していた。一説には、現在{{jalink|二宮神社 (柏原市)||二宮神社}}（柏原市安堂町19-9。大正時代に2社を合祀して現在地に遷座）のある地が「字白坂」であることから、元の宿奈川田神社の鎮座地であるという。そのため、二宮神社も式内・宿奈川田神社の論社とされているが、二宮神社では式内社とは称していない。 明治5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）に村社に列格し、白坂明神から現社名に改称した。 ===境内=== File:Sukunakawata-jinja, honden.jpg|本殿 File:Sukunakawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|末社 ===交通アクセス=== *{{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125|JR西日本}} {{jalink|関西本線|Q1199703|大和路線}} {{jalink|高井田駅 (大阪府柏原市)|Q856508|高井田駅}}（徒歩すぐ） === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===関連項目=== {{Commonscat|Sukunakawata-jinja}} *{{jalink|天湯川田神社|Q63891125}} *{{jalink|河内国の式内社一覧|Q11553385}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなかわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Sukunakawata Shrine''' (すくなかわたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kashiwara|Q873583}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. Locally it is commonly called “Shirasaka Shrine,” and maps/signs also use that name. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikone-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|Shinatobe-no-Mikoto}}. ''{{jalink|Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708}}'' and ''{{jalink|Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092}}'' state “deity unknown.” ===History=== The origin of foundation is unknown. Together with nearby {{jalink|Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125}} (Kashiwara Takaida 89), it is thought that the Tottori no Muraji, based in this area, enshrined their {{jalink|ancestral deity|Q85882782}}. In ''{{jalink|Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751}}'' “Tenjin Hongi,” {{jalink|Sukuna-hikone-no-Mikoto}} is given as ancestral deity of Tottori no Muraji. It had been an {{jalink|official shrine}} since the time of the {{jalink|Kōnin-shiki|Q17228444}}, and in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as “Sukunakawata Shrine, Ōagata District, Kawachi Province,” ranked as a small shrine. In the medieval period it was called “Shirasaka Daimyōjin.” One theory says the place now occupied by {{jalink|Ninomiya Shrine (Kashiwara)||Ninomiya Shrine}} (Andō-chō 19-9, moved to current site in Taishō after merging two shrines) was “Aza Shirasaka,” and was the original site of Sukunakawata Shrine. For that reason Ninomiya Shrine is also treated as a candidate shrine, though Ninomiya Shrine itself does not claim Shikinaisha status. In Meiji 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it was ranked as village shrine and renamed from Shirasaka Myōjin to the current shrine name. ===Precinct=== File:Sukunakawata-jinja, honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Sukunakawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine ===Access=== *{{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Kansai Main Line|Q1199703|Yamatoji Line}} {{jalink|Takaida Station (Kashiwara, Osaka)|Q856508|Takaida Station}} (immediate walking distance) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== {{Commonscat|Sukunakawata-jinja}} *{{jalink|Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125}} *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなかわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

In the medieval period it was called “Shirasaka Daimyōjin.” One theory says the place now occupied by {{jalink|Ninomiya Shrine (Kashiwara)||Ninomiya Shrine}} (Andō-chō 19-9, moved to current site in Taishō after merging two shrines) was “Aza Shirasaka,” and was the original site of Sukunakawata Shrine. For that reason Ninomiya Shrine is also treated as a candidate shrine, though Ninomiya Shrine itself does not claim Shikinaisha status. In Meiji 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it was ranked as village shrine and renamed from Shirasaka Myōjin to the current shrine name. ===Precinct=== File:Sukunakawata-jinja, honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Sukunakawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine ===Access=== *{{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Kansai Main Line|Q1199703|Yamatoji Line}} {{jalink|Takaida Station (Kashiwara, Osaka)|Q856508|Takaida Station}} (immediate walking distance) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== {{Commonscat|Sukunakawata-jinja}} *{{jalink|Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125}} *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなかわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|July 23|Q2722}} | 別名=Shirasaka Daimyōjin | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sukunakawata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Sukunakawata Shrine''' (すくなかわたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kashiwara|Q873583}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. Locally it is commonly called “Shirasaka Shrine,” and maps/signs also use that name. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikone-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|Shinatobe-no-Mikoto}}. ''{{jalink|Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708}}'' and ''{{jalink|Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092}}'' state “deity unknown.” ===History=== The origin of foundation is unknown. Together with nearby {{jalink|Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125}} (Kashiwara Takaida 89), it is thought that the Tottori no Muraji, based in this area, enshrined their {{jalink|ancestral deity|Q85882782}}. In ''{{jalink|Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751}}'' “Tenjin Hongi,” {{jalink|Sukuna-hikone-no-Mikoto}} is given as ancestral deity of Tottori no Muraji. It had been an {{jalink|official shrine}} since the time of the {{jalink|Kōnin-shiki|Q17228444}}, and in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as “Sukunakawata Shrine, Ōagata District, Kawachi Province,” ranked as a small shrine. In the medieval period it was called “Shirasaka Daimyōjin.” One theory says the place now occupied by {{jalink|Ninomiya Shrine (Kashiwara)||Ninomiya Shrine}} (Andō-chō 19-9, moved to current site in Taishō after merging two shrines) was “Aza Shirasaka,” and was the original site of Sukunakawata Shrine. For that reason Ninomiya Shrine is also treated as a candidate shrine, though Ninomiya Shrine itself does not claim Shikinaisha status. In Meiji 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it was ranked as village shrine and renamed from Shirasaka Myōjin to the current shrine name. ===Precinct=== File:Sukunakawata-jinja, honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Sukunakawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine ===Access=== *{{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Kansai Main Line|Q1199703|Yamatoji Line}} {{jalink|Takaida Station (Kashiwara, Osaka)|Q856508|Takaida Station}} (immediate walking distance) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== {{Commonscat|Sukunakawata-jinja}} *{{jalink|Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125}} *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなかわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

Haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Kashiwara|Q873583}} Takaida 661 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikone-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|Shinatobe-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|July 23|Q2722}} | 別名=Shirasaka Daimyōjin | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sukunakawata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Sukunakawata Shrine''' (すくなかわたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kashiwara|Q873583}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. Locally it is commonly called “Shirasaka Shrine,” and maps/signs also use that name. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikone-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|Shinatobe-no-Mikoto}}. ''{{jalink|Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708}}'' and ''{{jalink|Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092}}'' state “deity unknown.” ===History=== The origin of foundation is unknown. Together with nearby {{jalink|Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125}} (Kashiwara Takaida 89), it is thought that the Tottori no Muraji, based in this area, enshrined their {{jalink|ancestral deity|Q85882782}}. In ''{{jalink|Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751}}'' “Tenjin Hongi,” {{jalink|Sukuna-hikone-no-Mikoto}} is given as ancestral deity of Tottori no Muraji. It had been an {{jalink|official shrine}} since the time of the {{jalink|Kōnin-shiki|Q17228444}}, and in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as “Sukunakawata Shrine, Ōagata District, Kawachi Province,” ranked as a small shrine. In the medieval period it was called “Shirasaka Daimyōjin.” One theory says the place now occupied by {{jalink|Ninomiya Shrine (Kashiwara)||Ninomiya Shrine}} (Andō-chō 19-9, moved to current site in Taishō after merging two shrines) was “Aza Shirasaka,” and was the original site of Sukunakawata Shrine. For that reason Ninomiya Shrine is also treated as a candidate shrine, though Ninomiya Shrine itself does not claim Shikinaisha status. In Meiji 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it was ranked as village shrine and renamed from Shirasaka Myōjin to the current shrine name. ===Precinct=== File:Sukunakawata-jinja, honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Sukunakawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine ===Access=== *{{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Kansai Main Line|Q1199703|Yamatoji Line}} {{jalink|Takaida Station (Kashiwara, Osaka)|Q856508|Takaida Station}} (immediate walking distance) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== {{Commonscat|Sukunakawata-jinja}} *{{jalink|Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125}} *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなかわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]][[Category:qq]]

Shrine | 画像=[[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583}} Takaida 661 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikone-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Takamimusubi||WD=|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=July 23|Q2722|WD=Q2722}} | 別名=Shirasaka Daimyōjin | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sukunakawata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Sukunakawata Shrine''' (すくなかわたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. Locally it is commonly called “Shirasaka Shrine,” and maps/signs also use that name. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikone-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takamimusubi||WD=|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto}}. ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708}}'' and ''{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092}}'' state “deity unknown.” ===History=== The origin of foundation is unknown. Together with nearby {{ill|lt=Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125|WD=Q63891125}} (Kashiwara Takaida 89), it is thought that the Tottori no Muraji, based in this area, enshrined their {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782}}. In ''{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751}}'' “Tenjin Hongi,” {{ill||lt=Sukuna-hikone-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} is given as ancestral deity of Tottori no Muraji. It had been an {{ill||lt=official shrine|UNKNOWN}} since the time of the {{ill|lt=Kōnin-shiki|Q17228444|WD=Q17228444}}, and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} it is listed as “Sukunakawata Shrine, Ōagata District, Kawachi Province,” ranked as a small shrine. In the medieval period it was called “Shirasaka Daimyōjin.” One theory says the place now occupied by {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Shrine (Kashiwara)||WD=|lt=Ninomiya Shrine}} (Andō-chō 19-9, moved to current site in Taishō after merging two shrines) was “Aza Shirasaka,” and was the original site of Sukunakawata Shrine. For that reason Ninomiya Shrine is also treated as a candidate shrine, though Ninomiya Shrine itself does not claim Shikinaisha status. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), it was ranked as village shrine and renamed from Shirasaka Myōjin to the current shrine name. ===Precinct=== File:Sukunakawata-jinja, honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Sukunakawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine ===Access=== *{{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} {{ill|lt=Kansai Main Line|Q1199703|WD=Q1199703|lt=Yamatoji Line}} {{ill|lt=Takaida Station (Kashiwara, Osaka)|Q856508|WD=Q856508|lt=Takaida Station}} (immediate walking distance) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== {{Commonscat|Sukunakawata-jinja}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125|WD=Q63891125}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなかわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Shrine | 画像=[[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583}} Takaida 661 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikone-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Takamimusubi||WD=|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=July 23|Q2722|WD=Q2722}} | 別名=Shirasaka Daimyōjin | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sukunakawata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Sukunakawata Shrine''' (すくなかわたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. Locally it is commonly called “Shirasaka Shrine,” and maps/signs also use that name. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikone-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takamimusubi||WD=|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto}}. ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708}}'' and ''{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092}}'' state “deity unknown.” ===History=== The origin of foundation is unknown. Together with nearby {{ill|lt=Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125|WD=Q63891125}} (Kashiwara Takaida 89), it is thought that the Tottori no Muraji, based in this area, enshrined their {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782}}. In ''{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751}}'' “Tenjin Hongi,” {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukuna-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} is given as ancestral deity of Tottori no Muraji. It had been an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} since the time of the {{ill|lt=Kōnin-shiki|Q17228444|WD=Q17228444}}, and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} it is listed as “Sukunakawata Shrine, Ōagata District, Kawachi Province,” ranked as a small shrine. In the medieval period it was called “Shirasaka Daimyōjin.” One theory says the place now occupied by {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Shrine (Kashiwara)||WD=|lt=Ninomiya Shrine}} (Andō-chō 19-9, moved to current site in Taishō after merging two shrines) was “Aza Shirasaka,” and was the original site of Sukunakawata Shrine. For that reason Ninomiya Shrine is also treated as a candidate shrine, though Ninomiya Shrine itself does not claim Shikinaisha status. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), it was ranked as village shrine and renamed from Shirasaka Myōjin to the current shrine name. ===Precinct=== File:Sukunakawata-jinja, honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Sukunakawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine ===Access=== *{{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} {{ill|lt=Kansai Main Line|Q1199703|WD=Q1199703|lt=Yamatoji Line}} {{ill|lt=Takaida Station (Kashiwara, Osaka)|Q856508|WD=Q856508|lt=Takaida Station}} (immediate walking distance) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== {{Commonscat|Sukunakawata-jinja}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125|WD=Q63891125}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなかわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Shrine | 画像=[[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583}} Takaida 661 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikone-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=July 23|Q2722|WD=Q2722}} | 別名=Shirasaka Daimyōjin | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sukunakawata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Sukunakawata Shrine''' (すくなかわたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. Locally it is commonly called “Shirasaka Shrine,” and maps/signs also use that name. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikone-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto}}. ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708}}'' and ''{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092}}'' state “deity unknown.” ===History=== The origin of foundation is unknown. Together with nearby {{ill|lt=Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125|WD=Q63891125}} (Kashiwara Takaida 89), it is thought that the Tottori no Muraji, based in this area, enshrined their {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782}}. In ''{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751}}'' “Tenjin Hongi,” {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukuna-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} is given as ancestral deity of Tottori no Muraji. It had been an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} since the time of the {{ill|lt=Kōnin-shiki|Q17228444|WD=Q17228444}}, and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} it is listed as “Sukunakawata Shrine, Ōagata District, Kawachi Province,” ranked as a small shrine. In the medieval period it was called “Shirasaka Daimyōjin.” One theory says the place now occupied by {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Shrine (Kashiwara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Ninomiya Shrine}} (Andō-chō 19-9, moved to current site in Taishō after merging two shrines) was “Aza Shirasaka,” and was the original site of Sukunakawata Shrine. For that reason Ninomiya Shrine is also treated as a candidate shrine, though Ninomiya Shrine itself does not claim Shikinaisha status. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), it was ranked as village shrine and renamed from Shirasaka Myōjin to the current shrine name. ===Precinct=== File:Sukunakawata-jinja, honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Sukunakawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine ===Access=== *{{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} {{ill|lt=Kansai Main Line|Q1199703|WD=Q1199703|lt=Yamatoji Line}} {{ill|lt=Takaida Station (Kashiwara, Osaka)|Q856508|WD=Q856508|lt=Takaida Station}} (immediate walking distance) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== {{Commonscat|Sukunakawata-jinja}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125|WD=Q63891125}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなかわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'大阪府柏原市高井田661番地', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sukunakawata-jinja}} [[JA:宿奈川田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11455616}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=April 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Sukunakawata Shrine | 画像=[[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583}} Takaida 661 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikone-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=July 23|Q2722|WD=Q2722}} | 別名=Shirasaka Daimyōjin | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sukunakawata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Sukunakawata Shrine''' (すくなかわたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. Locally it is commonly called “Shirasaka Shrine,” and maps/signs also use that name. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikone-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto}}. ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708}}'' and ''{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092}}'' state “deity unknown.” ===History=== The origin of foundation is unknown. Together with nearby {{ill|lt=Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125|WD=Q63891125}} (Kashiwara Takaida 89), it is thought that the Tottori no Muraji, based in this area, enshrined their {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782}}. In ''{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751}}'' “Tenjin Hongi,” {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukuna-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} is given as ancestral deity of Tottori no Muraji. It had been an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} since the time of the {{ill|lt=Kōnin-shiki|Q17228444|WD=Q17228444}}, and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} it is listed as “Sukunakawata Shrine, Ōagata District, Kawachi Province,” ranked as a small shrine. In the medieval period it was called “Shirasaka Daimyōjin.” One theory says the place now occupied by {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Shrine (Kashiwara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Ninomiya Shrine}} (Andō-chō 19-9, moved to current site in Taishō after merging two shrines) was “Aza Shirasaka,” and was the original site of Sukunakawata Shrine. For that reason Ninomiya Shrine is also treated as a candidate shrine, though Ninomiya Shrine itself does not claim Shikinaisha status. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), it was ranked as village shrine and renamed from Shirasaka Myōjin to the current shrine name. ===Precinct=== File:Sukunakawata-jinja, honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Sukunakawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine ===Access=== *{{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} {{ill|lt=Kansai Main Line|Q1199703|WD=Q1199703|lt=Yamatoji Line}} {{ill|lt=Takaida Station (Kashiwara, Osaka)|Q856508|WD=Q856508|lt=Takaida Station}} (immediate walking distance) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== {{Commonscat|Sukunakawata-jinja}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125|WD=Q63891125}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなかわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

画像=[[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583|1=Kashiwara}} Takaida 661 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikone-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}

{{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|1=Shinatsuhiko}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=July 23|Q2722|WD=Q2722|1=July 23}} | 別名=Shirasaka Daimyōjin | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sukunakawata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Sukunakawata Shrine''' (すくなかわたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583|1=Kashiwara}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Locally it is commonly called “Shirasaka Shrine,” and maps/signs also use that name. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikone-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}}, and {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|1=Shinatsuhiko}}. ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}'' and ''{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}'' state “deity unknown.” ===History=== The origin of foundation is unknown. Together with nearby {{ill|lt=Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125|WD=Q63891125|1=Amayukawata Shrine}} (Kashiwara Takaida 89), it is thought that the Tottori no Muraji, based in this area, enshrined their {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782|1=Oyagami}}. In ''{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}'' “Tenjin Hongi,” {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukuna-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} is given as ancestral deity of Tottori no Muraji. It had been an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} since the time of the {{ill|lt=Kōnin-shiki|Q17228444|WD=Q17228444|1=Kōnin-shiki}}, and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as “Sukunakawata Shrine, Ōagata District, Kawachi Province,” ranked as a small shrine. In the medieval period it was called “Shirasaka Daimyōjin.” One theory says the place now occupied by {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Shrine (Kashiwara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Ninomiya Shrine}} (Andō-chō 19-9, moved to current site in Taishō after merging two shrines) was “Aza Shirasaka,” and was the original site of Sukunakawata Shrine. For that reason Ninomiya Shrine is also treated as a candidate shrine, though Ninomiya Shrine itself does not claim Shikinaisha status. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as village shrine and renamed from Shirasaka Myōjin to the current shrine name. ===Precinct=== File:Sukunakawata-jinja, honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Sukunakawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine ===Access=== *{{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Kansai Main Line|Q1199703|WD=Q1199703|lt=Yamatoji Line|1=Kansai Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Takaida Station (Kashiwara, Osaka)|Q856508|WD=Q856508|lt=Takaida Station|1=Takaida Station (Osaka)}} (immediate walking distance) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== {{Commonscat|Sukunakawata-jinja}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125|WD=Q63891125|1=Amayukawata Shrine}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなかわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

''{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}'' and ''{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}'' state “deity unknown.” ===History=== The origin of foundation is unknown. In ''{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}'' “Tenjin Hongi,” {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukuna-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} is given as ancestral deity of Tottori no Muraji. It had been an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} since the time of the {{ill|lt=Kōnin-shiki|Q17228444|WD=Q17228444|1=Kōnin-shiki}}, and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as “Sukunakawata Shrine, Ōagata District, Kawachi Province,” ranked as a small shrine. In the medieval period it was called “Shirasaka Daimyōjin.” One theory says the place now occupied by {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Shrine (Kashiwara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Ninomiya Shrine}} (Andō-chō 19-9, moved to current site in Taishō after merging two shrines) was “Aza Shirasaka,” and was the original site of Sukunakawata Shrine. For that reason Ninomiya Shrine is also treated as a candidate shrine, though Ninomiya Shrine itself does not claim Shikinaisha status. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as village shrine and renamed from Shirasaka Myōjin to the current shrine name. ===Precinct=== File:Sukunakawata-jinja, honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Sukunakawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine ===Access=== *{{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Kansai Main Line|Q1199703|WD=Q1199703|lt=Yamatoji Line|1=Kansai Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Takaida Station (Kashiwara, Osaka)|Q856508|WD=Q856508|lt=Takaida Station|1=Takaida Station (Osaka)}} (immediate walking distance) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== {{Commonscat|Sukunakawata-jinja}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125|WD=Q63891125|1=Amayukawata Shrine}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなかわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=July 23|Q2722|WD=Q2722|1=July 23}} | 別名=Shirasaka Daimyōjin | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sukunakawata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Sukunakawata Shrine''' (すくなかわたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583|1=Kashiwara}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Locally it is commonly called “Shirasaka Shrine,” and maps/signs also use that name. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikone-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}}, and {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|1=Shinatsuhiko}}. ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}'' and ''{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}'' state “deity unknown.” ===History=== The origin of foundation is unknown. Together with nearby {{ill|lt=Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125|WD=Q63891125|1=Amayukawata Shrine}} (Kashiwara Takaida 89), it is thought that the Tottori no Muraji, based in this area, enshrined their {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782|1=Oyagami}}. In ''{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}'' “Tenjin Hongi,” {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukuna-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} is given as ancestral deity of Tottori no Muraji. It had been an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} since the time of the {{ill|lt=Kōnin-shiki|Q17228444|WD=Q17228444|1=Kōnin-shiki}}, and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as “Sukunakawata Shrine, Ōagata District, Kawachi Province,” ranked as a small shrine. In the medieval period it was called “Shirasaka Daimyōjin.” One theory says the place now occupied by {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Shrine (Kashiwara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Ninomiya Shrine}} (Andō-chō 19-9, moved to current site in Taishō after merging two shrines) was “Aza Shirasaka,” and was the original site of Sukunakawata Shrine. For that reason Ninomiya Shrine is also treated as a candidate shrine, though Ninomiya Shrine itself does not claim Shikinaisha status. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as village shrine and renamed from Shirasaka Myōjin to the current shrine name. ===Precinct=== File:Sukunakawata-jinja, honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Sukunakawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine ===Access=== *{{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Kansai Main Line|Q1199703|WD=Q1199703|lt=Yamatoji Line|1=Kansai Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Takaida Station (Kashiwara, Osaka)|Q856508|WD=Q856508|lt=Takaida Station|1=Takaida Station (Osaka)}} (immediate walking distance) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== {{Commonscat|Sukunakawata-jinja}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125|WD=Q63891125|1=Amayukawata Shrine}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すくなかわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]][[Category:qq]]

Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Sukunakawata Shrine''' (すくなかわたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583|1=Kashiwara}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Locally it is commonly called “Shirasaka Shrine,” and maps/signs also use that name. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikone-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}}, and {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|1=Shinatsuhiko}}. ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}'' and ''{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}'' state “deity unknown.” ===History=== The origin of foundation is unknown. Together with nearby {{ill|lt=Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125|WD=Q63891125|1=Amayukawata Shrine}} (Kashiwara Takaida 89), it is thought that the Tottori no Muraji, based in this area, enshrined their {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782|1=Oyagami}}. In ''{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}'' “Tenjin Hongi,” {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukuna-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} is given as ancestral deity of Tottori no Muraji. It had been an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} since the time of the {{ill|lt=Kōnin-shiki|Q17228444|WD=Q17228444|1=Kōnin-shiki}}, and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as “Sukunakawata Shrine, Ōagata District, Kawachi Province,” ranked as a small shrine. In the medieval period it was called “Shirasaka Daimyōjin.” One theory says the place now occupied by {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Shrine (Kashiwara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Ninomiya Shrine}} (Andō-chō 19-9, moved to current site in Taishō after merging two shrines) was “Aza Shirasaka,” and was the original site of Sukunakawata Shrine. For that reason Ninomiya Shrine is also treated as a candidate shrine, though Ninomiya Shrine itself does not claim Shikinaisha status. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as village shrine and renamed from Shirasaka Myōjin to the current shrine name. ===Precinct=== File:Sukunakawata-jinja, honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Sukunakawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine ===Access=== *{{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Kansai Main Line|Q1199703|WD=Q1199703|lt=Yamatoji Line|1=Kansai Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Takaida Station (Kashiwara, Osaka)|Q856508|WD=Q856508|lt=Takaida Station|1=Takaida Station (Osaka)}} (immediate walking distance) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== {{Commonscat|Sukunakawata-jinja}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125|WD=Q63891125|1=Amayukawata Shrine}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]][[Category:qq]]

画像=[[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583|1=Kashiwara}} Takaida 661 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikone-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}
{{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|1=Shinatsuhiko}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=July 23|Q2722|WD=Q2722|1=July 23}} | 別名=Shirasaka Daimyōjin | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sukunakawata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Sukunakawata Shrine''' (すくなかわたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583|1=Kashiwara}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Locally it is commonly called “Shirasaka Shrine,” and maps/signs also use that name. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikone-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}}, and {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|1=Shinatsuhiko}}. ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}'' and ''{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}'' state “deity unknown.” ===History=== The origin of foundation is unknown. Together with nearby {{ill|lt=Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125|WD=Q63891125|1=Amayukawata Shrine}} (Kashiwara Takaida 89), it is thought that the Tottori no Muraji, based in this area, enshrined their {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782|1=Oyagami}}. In ''{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}'' “Tenjin Hongi,” {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukuna-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} is given as ancestral deity of Tottori no Muraji. It had been an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} since the time of the {{ill|lt=Kōnin-shiki|Q17228444|WD=Q17228444|1=Kōnin-shiki}}, and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as “Sukunakawata Shrine, Ōagata District, Kawachi Province,” ranked as a small shrine. In the medieval period it was called “Shirasaka Daimyōjin.” One theory says the place now occupied by {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Shrine (Kashiwara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Ninomiya Shrine}} (Andō-chō 19-9, moved to current site in Taishō after merging two shrines) was “Aza Shirasaka,” and was the original site of Sukunakawata Shrine. For that reason Ninomiya Shrine is also treated as a candidate shrine, though Ninomiya Shrine itself does not claim Shikinaisha status. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as village shrine and renamed from Shirasaka Myōjin to the current shrine name. ===Precinct=== File:Sukunakawata-jinja, honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Sukunakawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine ===Access=== *{{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Kansai Main Line|Q1199703|WD=Q1199703|lt=Yamatoji Line|1=Kansai Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Takaida Station (Kashiwara, Osaka)|Q856508|WD=Q856508|lt=Takaida Station|1=Takaida Station (Osaka)}} (immediate walking distance) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== {{Commonscat|Sukunakawata-jinja}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125|WD=Q63891125|1=Amayukawata Shrine}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:柏原市の建築物]][[Category:qq]]

Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583|1=Kashiwara}} Takaida 661 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikone-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}
{{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|1=Shinatsuhiko}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=July 23|Q2722|WD=Q2722|1=July 23}} | 別名=Shirasaka Daimyōjin | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sukunakawata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Sukunakawata Shrine''' (すくなかわたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583|1=Kashiwara}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Locally it is commonly called “Shirasaka Shrine,” and maps/signs also use that name. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikone-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}}, and {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|1=Shinatsuhiko}}. ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}'' and ''{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}'' state “deity unknown.” ===History=== The origin of foundation is unknown. Together with nearby {{ill|lt=Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125|WD=Q63891125|1=Amayukawata Shrine}} (Kashiwara Takaida 89), it is thought that the Tottori no Muraji, based in this area, enshrined their {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782|1=Oyagami}}. In ''{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}'' “Tenjin Hongi,” {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukuna-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} is given as ancestral deity of Tottori no Muraji. It had been an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} since the time of the {{ill|lt=Kōnin-shiki|Q17228444|WD=Q17228444|1=Kōnin-shiki}}, and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as “Sukunakawata Shrine, Ōagata District, Kawachi Province,” ranked as a small shrine. In the medieval period it was called “Shirasaka Daimyōjin.” One theory says the place now occupied by {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Shrine (Kashiwara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Ninomiya Shrine}} (Andō-chō 19-9, moved to current site in Taishō after merging two shrines) was “Aza Shirasaka,” and was the original site of Sukunakawata Shrine. For that reason Ninomiya Shrine is also treated as a candidate shrine, though Ninomiya Shrine itself does not claim Shikinaisha status. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as village shrine and renamed from Shirasaka Myōjin to the current shrine name. ===Precinct=== File:Sukunakawata-jinja, honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Sukunakawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine ===Access=== *{{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Kansai Main Line|Q1199703|WD=Q1199703|lt=Yamatoji Line|1=Kansai Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Takaida Station (Kashiwara, Osaka)|Q856508|WD=Q856508|lt=Takaida Station|1=Takaida Station (Osaka)}} (immediate walking distance) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== {{Commonscat|Sukunakawata-jinja}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125|WD=Q63891125|1=Amayukawata Shrine}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashiwara, Osaka]][[Category:qq]]

For that reason Ninomiya Shrine is also treated as a candidate shrine, though Ninomiya Shrine itself does not claim Shikinaisha status.
