Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583|1=Kashiwara}} Takaida 661 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikone-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}
{{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|1=Shinatsuhiko}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=July 23|Q2722|WD=Q2722|1=July 23}} | 別名=Shirasaka Daimyōjin | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sukunakawata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sukunakawata-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Sukunakawata Shrine''' (すくなかわたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashiwara|Q873583|WD=Q873583|1=Kashiwara}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Locally it is commonly called “Shirasaka Shrine,” and maps/signs also use that name. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikone-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}}, and {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|1=Shinatsuhiko}}. ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}'' and ''{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}'' state “deity unknown.” ===History=== The origin of foundation is unknown. Together with nearby {{ill|lt=Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125|WD=Q63891125|1=Amayukawata Shrine}} (Kashiwara Takaida 89), it is thought that the Tottori no Muraji, based in this area, enshrined their {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782|1=Oyagami}}. In ''{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}'' “Tenjin Hongi,” {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukuna-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} is given as ancestral deity of Tottori no Muraji. It had been an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} since the time of the {{ill|lt=Kōnin-shiki|Q17228444|WD=Q17228444|1=Kōnin-shiki}}, and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as “Sukunakawata Shrine, Ōagata District, Kawachi Province,” ranked as a small shrine. In the medieval period it was called “Shirasaka Daimyōjin.” One theory says the place now occupied by {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Shrine (Kashiwara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Ninomiya Shrine}} (Andō-chō 19-9, moved to current site in Taishō after merging two shrines) was “Aza Shirasaka,” and was the original site of Sukunakawata Shrine. For that reason Ninomiya Shrine is also treated as a candidate shrine, though Ninomiya Shrine itself does not claim Shikinaisha status. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as village shrine and renamed from Shirasaka Myōjin to the current shrine name. ===Precinct=== File:Sukunakawata-jinja, honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Sukunakawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine ===Access=== *{{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Kansai Main Line|Q1199703|WD=Q1199703|lt=Yamatoji Line|1=Kansai Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Takaida Station (Kashiwara, Osaka)|Q856508|WD=Q856508|lt=Takaida Station|1=Takaida Station (Osaka)}} (immediate walking distance) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== {{Commonscat|Sukunakawata-jinja}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125|WD=Q63891125|1=Amayukawata Shrine}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashiwara, Osaka]]

Locally it is commonly called “Shirasaka Shrine,” and maps/signs also use that name. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikone-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}}, and {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatobe-no-Mikoto|1=Shinatsuhiko}}. ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}'' and ''{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}'' state “deity unknown.” ===History=== The origin of foundation is unknown. Together with nearby {{ill|lt=Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125|WD=Q63891125|1=Amayukawata Shrine}} (Kashiwara Takaida 89), it is thought that the Tottori no Muraji, based in this area, enshrined their {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782|1=Oyagami}}. In ''{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}'' “Tenjin Hongi,” {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukuna-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} is given as ancestral deity of Tottori no Muraji. It had been an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} since the time of the {{ill|lt=Kōnin-shiki|Q17228444|WD=Q17228444|1=Kōnin-shiki}}, and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as “Sukunakawata Shrine, Ōagata District, Kawachi Province,” ranked as a small shrine. In the medieval period it was called “Shirasaka Daimyōjin.” One theory says the place now occupied by {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Shrine (Kashiwara)|UNKNOWN|lt=Ninomiya Shrine}} (Andō-chō 19-9, moved to current site in Taishō after merging two shrines) was “Aza Shirasaka,” and was the original site of Sukunakawata Shrine. For that reason Ninomiya Shrine is also treated as a candidate shrine, though Ninomiya Shrine itself does not claim Shikinaisha status. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as village shrine and renamed from Shirasaka Myōjin to the current shrine name. ===Precinct=== File:Sukunakawata-jinja, honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Sukunakawata-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine ===Access=== *{{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Kansai Main Line|Q1199703|WD=Q1199703|lt=Yamatoji Line|1=Kansai Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Takaida Station (Kashiwara, Osaka)|Q856508|WD=Q856508|lt=Takaida Station|1=Takaida Station (Osaka)}} (immediate walking distance) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===Related items=== {{Commonscat|Sukunakawata-jinja}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-yukawata Shrine|Q63891125|WD=Q63891125|1=Amayukawata Shrine}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sukunakawata Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kashiwara, Osaka]]
